businessModel.text: Revenue from US Home Improvement retail (85%), Lowe's.com e-commerce (8%), and international/outlet (7%). Split approximately 55% DIY consumer, 45% professional contractor through Pro desks.

recentCatalysts.text: Pro-desk segment (>40% revenue) growing double-digit as contractor loyalty recovers TinyAI-driven inventory optimisation rolling out chainwide — reducing stockouts by 22% Lowe's.com marketplace launch Q4 2025 adding third-party SKUs Share-buyback programme authorised: $10bn authorised through FY2026
items: Pro-desk segment (>40% revenue) growing double-digit as contractor loyalty recovers
TinyAI-driven inventory optimisation rolling out chainwide — reducing stockouts by 22%
Lowe's.com marketplace launch Q4 2025 adding third-party SKUs
Share-buyback programme authorised: $10bn authorised through FY2026

keyRisks.text: 1. US housing-affordability crisis: existing-home sales down 15% YoY Jan 2026 2. Consumer spending shift from big-ticket DIY to repair/maintenance discretionary 3. Home Depot gaining share in pro-contractor segment — direct competitive pressure 4. tariff pressure on Chinese imported building materials (Lowe's sources ~45% Asia) 5. Tight labour market: instore service model requires 80k+ seasonal hires annually

risks.risk: 1. US housing-affordability crisis: existing-home sales down 15% YoY Jan 2026
2. Consumer spending shift from big-ticket DIY to repair/maintenance discretionary
3. Home Depot gaining share in pro-contractor segment — direct competitive pressure
4. tariff pressure on Chinese imported building materials (Lowe's sources ~45% Asia)
5. Tight labour market: instore service model requires 80k+ seasonal hires annually

whoShouldOwn.text: Home-improvement cyclical trade; best entry on housing-starts contraction signals. Pro-contractor differentiation is the structural tailwind. NOT suitable for risk-off macro positioning or investors needing near-term positive earnings revisions.

entryExit.text: Average in the $220–$230 range (10% above 52-week low). HOLD $230–$255. REDUCE above $255. Monitor: US existing-home sales monthly data as housing-demand proxy. | BUY: Average in the $220–$230 range (10% above 52-week low). | REDUCE above $255.

sources.marketData: Live quote data sourced via DYOR HQ data pipeline (Google Sheets + Yahoo Finance). Fields include price, market capitalisation, 52-week range, P/E, EPS, and volume.
sources.companyFilings: Public company filings, regulatory announcements, investor presentations, and RNS where referenced in the analysis.
